subject: Social Studies
canonical_name: Grade 7 Social Studies: Economics — Incentives and Networks
coherence_reason: The standards listed cover a range of loosely related topics including communication, technology, collaboration, banking, economics, and organizational purpose, indicating multiple distinct concepts rather than a single coherent one.

subject: Social Studies
sample_standards: Your child uses what they know about word parts and sentence structure to understand new words in harder reading.
Your child can explain and judge President Franklin Roosevelt’s actions and plans during the New Deal era.
Your child can explain how people used different technologies in the time period being studied.
Your child can use important ideas and patterns from history to help understand the past.
Your child can explain a speaker's argument and decide if the reasons and evidence make sense.
Your child can explain how changes in where people live affect how land is used in different places.
Your child can describe the world using maps and other geography tools.
Your child can understand and judge information shown in tables, graphs, maps, and texts.
canonical_name: Grade 7 Social Studies: Historical Analysis — Using Sources and Evidence
coherence_reason: The standards contain a wide range of unrelated topics including vocabulary, historical analysis, geography, technology, and critical thinking skills.

subject: Social Studies
sample_standards: This standard is not a separate requirement.

canonical_name: Grade 7 Social Studies: World Regions — Civilizations and Resources (400-1500s CE)
coherence_reason: The standards cluster together unrelated concepts: world regions and time periods, resource distribution, conflict, settlements, and map skills.
